jobs.title: EHS Manager Large Scale Operations 80-100% (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

The EHS Manager drives the successful implementation and continuous improvement of Environmental, Occupational Health, and Safety (EHS) programs in collaboration with assigned manufacturing facilities. This role serves as the primary EHS contact for Large Scale Plants in Visp, enabling safe, compliant, and sustainable chemical manufacturing.

What you will get

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.

• Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi 

What you will do:

• Serve as the main point of contact for EHS topics and ensure compliance with regulatory and internal requirements

• Support and conduct EHS risk assessments, including technical change evaluations

• Participate in Safety Gemba Walks, audits, inspections, and incident investigations, providing subject matter expertise

• Drive corrective and preventive actions (CAPA) and track progress on EHS initiatives

• Collaborate with Safety Coaches, including their selection, training, and ongoing support

• Deliver EHS trainings in line with the site’s training strategy and promote a strong safety culture

• Support workplace risk assessments, Personal Protective Equipment guidance, fire safety, and emergency response activities

What we are looking for:

• University degree (Bachelor/Master) in Pharmaceutical Technology, Chemistry, Process Engineering, Biotechnology, Molecular Biology, Microbiology or equivalent professional qualification

• Proven experience in pharmaceutical or chemical manufacturing environments

• Swiss safety specialist certification (EKAS, ASGS) or comparable EHS qualification

• Strong knowledge of Swiss safety regulations and occupational safety practices

• Excellent communication, moderation, and presentation skills

• Ability to lead safety discussions, incident investigations, and training programs

• High social competence and ability to influence safety culture; German is an advantage

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? 
Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Head of OE & PMO
jobs.description:
Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across three continents. While we work in science, there is no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is talented people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that is the kind of work we want to be part of.

The Head of OE & PMO leads the Operational Excellence and PMO agenda, driving business results aligned with the Dedicated to Excellence strategy. The role leads and develops a team of OE Business Partners and OE/PMO Managers, coaches the Mammalian Operations leadership team, and embeds Lean Six Sigma principles to sustain a culture of continuous improvement. Responsible for Hoshin planning, X‑Matrix deployment, Project Portfolio Management (PPM), and PMO governance, the role translates strategy into actionable operational excellence and innovation projects across functions to deliver sustainable performance improvement.

Key Responsibilities:

• Responsible for the OE & PMO strategy and PMO governance for Mammalian Operations Visp

• Define OE & PMO strategy for Mammalian Operations Visp, establish continuous improvement goals

• PMO governance for Tier 3 and 4 levels, supporting all LT topics

• Drive implementation of Lonza operational excellence roadmap, assessing progress and defining actions

• Align and prioritize innovation initiatives, and projects, ensuring synergies of local improvements with wider transformational objectives

• Managing a team of OE and PMO Managers on day-to-day basis

• Leading multidisciplinary execution teams

• Work with functional leaders to establish continuous improvement goals & act as a liaison with finance to build improvement budgets & validate actual improvement ROI results

• Review, report & improve metrics by implementing tracking systems for monitoring

• Work with the team to uncover and assess lean opportunities; helping to prioritize those projects with greatest benefit and support delivery with well executed sustainable steps

• Strengthen collaboration and best‑practice exchange within the global and local PMO & OE community across sites and within Visp

• Sustaining a culture for a successful, high-performance team. Maintains a culture of empowerment and innovation, trust, high performance and change readiness

• Mentor, coach or train team members including green belts in Lean / Lean Six Sigma methodologies

• Lead and facilitate improvement/problem solving workshops

• Performing other duties as assigned

Requirements: 

• Education: Bachelor’s degree in Process Engineering, Pharmaceutical Technology, Engineering or equivalent

• Experience:  10+ years in Biologics GMP manufacturing with operational experience 

• Leadership Skills: Demonstrated ability to lead teams, get results, and promote collaboration across departments

• Language Skills: Proficient in English (required); German proficiency is a plus

Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically.
People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the satisfaction that comes with improving lives all around the world. The satisfaction that comes with making a meaningful difference.

The role leads and develops a team of OE Business Partners and OE/PMO Managers, coaches the Mammalian Operations leadership team, and embeds Lean Six Sigma principles to sustain a culture of continuous improvement. Responsible for Hoshin planning, X‑Matrix deployment, Project Portfolio Management (PPM), and PMO governance, the role translates strategy into actionable operational excellence and innovation projects across functions to deliver sustainable performance improvement.

Key Responsibilities:

• Responsible for the OE & PMO strategy and PMO governance for Mammalian Operations Visp

• Define OE & PMO strategy for Mammalian Operations Visp, establish continuous improvement goals

• PMO governance for Tier 3 and 4 levels, supporting all LT topics

• Drive implementation of Lonza operational excellence roadmap, assessing progress and defining actions

• Align and prioritize innovation initiatives, and projects, ensuring synergies of local improvements with wider transformational objectives

• Managing a team of OE and PMO Managers on day-to-day basis

• Leading multidisciplinary execution teams

• Work with functional leaders to establish continuous improvement goals & act as a liaison with finance to build improvement budgets & validate actual improvement ROI results

• Review, report & improve metrics by implementing tracking systems for monitoring

• Work with the team to uncover and assess lean opportunities; helping to prioritize those projects with greatest benefit and support delivery with well executed sustainable steps

• Strengthen collaboration and best‑practice exchange within the global and local PMO & OE community across sites and within Visp

• Sustaining a culture for a successful, high-performance team. Maintains a culture of empowerment and innovation, trust, high performance and change readiness

• Mentor, coach or train team members including green belts in Lean / Lean Six Sigma methodologies

• Lead and facilitate improvement/problem solving workshops

• Performing other duties as assigned

Requirements: 

• Education: Bachelor’s degree in Process Engineering, Pharmaceutical Technology, Engineering or equivalent

• Experience:  10+ years in Biologics GMP manufacturing with operational experience 

• Leadership Skills: Demonstrated ability to lead teams, get results, and promote collaboration across departments

• Language Skills: Proficient in English (required); German proficiency is a plus

Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically.
People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the satisfaction that comes with improving lives all around the world. The satisfaction that comes with making a meaningful difference.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Associate Director, Asset Design and Innovation 80-100% (m/f/d)

titleByLocale.en: Associate Director, Asset Design and Innovation 80-100% (m/f/d)
titleByLocale.it: Associate Direttore, Asset Design and Innovation 80-100% (m/f/d)
titleByLocale.de: Associate Direktor, Asset Design and Innovation 80-100% (m/f/d)
titleByLocale.fr: Associate Directeur, Asset Design and Innovation 80-100% (m/f/d)

jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

Based in Visp , Switzerland, and working in close collaboration with cross-functional global teams, including Global Engineering, MSAT, Quality, and the Advanced Synthesis Strategy Team. The Associate Director, Asset Design and Innovation (m/f/d) will lead the development and implementation of innovative, cost-efficient, and compliant manufacturing solutions. Your focus will be on driving technological advancements and asset design to support strategic growth projects.

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.

• Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi

What you will do:

• Drive innovation in asset design and manufacturing technologies to ensure state-of-the-art, reliable, and cost-effective operations.

• Evaluate and implement solutions that optimize operational expenditure (OpEx) and lifecycle costs.

• Create and own the respective business case

• Ensure integration of solutions into strategic growth projects while maintaining cGMP compliance and adherence to safety standards

• Share best practices and establish a manufacturing innovation platform across the Advanced Synthesis network.

• Support the Director of Operations in defining and executing strategic transformation initiatives.

What we are looking for:

• Master’s degree in Engineering, Chemistry, Biologics, or a related scientific discipline.

• Proven experience in leading complex projects in a matrix organization within a manufacturing or engineering environment.

• Strong knowledge in technology, engineering, regulatory compliance, and operational safety.

• Demonstrated experience managing project budgets and delivering against performance targets.

• Innovative mindset with the ability to think beyond traditional boundaries; experience with Lonza systems and processes is a plus.

• Fluent in English; German proficiency is a plus.

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.
Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? 
Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Senior MSAT Specialist, Small Molecules 80-100% (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

In this senior MSAT role based in Visp, Switzerland, you will play a key part in safeguarding product quality and patient safety by driving risk‑based impurity compliance for Small Molecules. You will lead impurity risk assessments and shape robust, pragmatic control strategies across the entire product lifecycle, in line with ICH Q3D, ICH Q3C and ICH Q9.

This position offers a high level of visibility and impact at a strategically important manufacturing site. You will work at the interface of MSAT, Compliance and Operations, collaborate closely with cross‑functional teams, influence critical decisions, and actively contribute to establishing sustainable compliance standards within a dynamic GMP environment.

What you will get

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.

• Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi 

What you will do:

• Lead and execute impurity risk assessments across the product lifecycle, identifying, assessing and documenting risks and mitigation measures

• Define and document scientifically sound, compliant impurity control strategies, including risk‑based testing concepts, acceptance criteria, sampling approaches and periodic reviews

• Evaluate manufacturing processes, utilities, equipment contact materials, raw materials, process aids and changes for potential impurity and carryover risks in a GMP environment

• Act as MSAT subject matter expert for compliance topics in cross‑functional teams, supporting technology transfers, deviations, investigations and change controls

• Identify compliance gaps and product impact, providing clear recommendations and pragmatic mitigation plans

• Ensure high‑quality, audit‑ and inspection‑ready documentation for internal governance and regulatory interactions

• Build strong collaboration with QC, QA, Manufacturing, Procurement, EHS and external partners to ensure robust data packages and sustainable control strategies

What we are looking for:

• University degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Pharmaceutical Sciences or a comparable discipline

• Solid experience in a GMP‑regulated environment, ideally within Small Molecule API or pharmaceutical manufacturing, MSAT, Process Development, QA or QC

• Strong ability to interpret process and analytical data and identify gaps against quality and compliance standards

• Excellent technical writing skills, with a focus on clear, concise and inspection‑ready documentation

• Confident communicator with the ability to align stakeholders and challenge constructively when needed

• High level of collaboration combined with autonomy, accountability and a pragmatic mindset

• Fluency in English; German is considered an advantage

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? 
Apply now.

• Build strong collaboration with QC, QA, Manufacturing, Procurement, EHS and external partners to ensure robust data packages and sustainable control strategies

What we are looking for:

• University degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Pharmaceutical Sciences or a comparable discipline

• Solid experience in a GMP‑regulated environment, ideally within Small Molecule API or pharmaceutical manufacturing, MSAT, Process Development, QA or QC

• Strong ability to interpret process and analytical data and identify gaps against quality and compliance standards

• Excellent technical writing skills, with a focus on clear, concise and inspection‑ready documentation

• Confident communicator with the ability to align stakeholders and challenge constructively when needed

• High level of collaboration combined with autonomy, accountability and a pragmatic mindset

• Fluency in English; German is considered an advantage

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? 
Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.
descriptionByLocale.de:
The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

In this senior MSAT role based in Visp, Switzerland, you will play a key part in safeguarding product quality and patient safety by driving risk‑based impurity compliance for Small Molecules. You will lead impurity risk assessments and shape robust, pragmatic control strategies across the entire product lifecycle, in line with ICH Q3D, ICH Q3C and ICH Q9.

This position offers a high level of visibility and impact at a strategically important manufacturing site. You will work at the interface of MSAT, Compliance and Operations, collaborate closely with cross‑functional teams, influence critical decisions, and actively contribute to establishing sustainable compliance standards within a dynamic GMP environment.

What you will get

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.

• Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi 

What you will do:

• Lead and execute impurity risk assessments across the product lifecycle, identifying, assessing and documenting risks and mitigation measures

• Define and document scientifically sound, compliant impurity control strategies, including risk‑based testing concepts, acceptance criteria, sampling approaches and periodic reviews

• Evaluate manufacturing processes, utilities, equipment contact materials, raw materials, process aids and changes for potential impurity and carryover risks in a GMP environment

• Act as MSAT subject matter expert for compliance topics in cross‑functional teams, supporting technology transfers, deviations, investigations and change controls

• Identify compliance gaps and product impact, providing clear recommendations and pragmatic mitigation plans

• Ensure high‑quality, audit‑ and inspection‑ready documentation for internal governance and regulatory interactions

• Build strong collaboration with QC, QA, Manufacturing, Procurement, EHS and external partners to ensure robust data packages and sustainable control strategies

What we are looking for:

• University degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Pharmaceutical Sciences or a comparable discipline

• Solid experience in a GMP‑regulated environment, ideally within Small Molecule API or pharmaceutical manufacturing, MSAT, Process Development, QA or QC

• Strong ability to interpret process and analytical data and identify gaps against quality and compliance standards

• Excellent technical writing skills, with a focus on clear, concise and inspection‑ready documentation

• Confident communicator with the ability to align stakeholders and challenge constructively when needed

• High level of collaboration combined with autonomy, accountability and a pragmatic mindset

• Fluency in English; German is considered an advantage

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. jobs.title: Brandschutzexperte (m/w/d)
jobs.description:
Lonza ist heute ein weltweit führendes Unternehmen im Bereich Life Sciences, welches auf drei Kontinenten tätig ist. Wir sind in der Wissenschaft tätig, jedoch gibt es keine Zauberformel dafür, wie wir die Arbeit angehen. Unsere wichtigste wissenschaftliche Lösung sind talentierte Mitarbeitende, die zusammenarbeiten und Ideen entwickeln, die anderen Unternehmen helfen, Menschen zu helfen. Im Gegenzug steuern unsere Mitarbeitenden ihre Karriere selbstständig. Denn ihre Ideen, ob gross und klein, verbessern die Welt. Und das ist die Art von Arbeit, an der wir Teil haben möchten.

Am Standort Visp  wird ein/e Brandschutzfachmann/frau / experte/in gesucht. In dieser Position werden Sie als selbstständige/r Fachperson für die Sicherstellung der Einhaltung aller Brandschutzgesetze und Richtlinien auf dem Werksareal verantwortlich sein. Sie stellen die Koordination der verschiedenen Fachbereiche und Anspruchsgruppen (z.B. Projektleiter, Betriebsleiter) im Rahmen der Gesetzgebung sicher. Sie beraten Projektteams und führen nach Verfügbarkeit selbstständig QS Brandschutz Projekte durch. Sie definieren anhand der Vorgaben, den organisatorischen BS und weisen die Verantwortlichen Betreiber auf die Umsetzung hin. Durch sporadische interne Audits, überprüfen Sie die Umsetzung durch die Betreiber.

Ihre Aufgaben:

• Verantwortlich für die Sicherstellung des Brandschutzes

• Sicherstellung der Koordination aller internen und externen Fachbereiche Brandschutz (Behörden, Ereignisdienste, Fachplaner, Engeneering usw.) untereinander

• Sicherstellung der Werksinternen Brandschutzmassnahmen im Rahmen der Businesskontinuität, der Vorgaben von Versicherern, Vorgaben aus technischen Richtlinien (inkl. TRCI)

• Beratung von Projektteams, Bauherrschaften, Brandschutz- und Fachplanern im Rahmen der Planung, Ausführung, Abnahme, Sicherstellung der Betriebsbereitschaft und der Wartungen

• Führt selbstständig BS Audits durch und definiert Massnahmen für die Betreiber

Ihr Anforderungsprofil:

• Brandschutzexperte/in VKF oder gleichwertige Ausbildung oder Brandschutzfachmann/frau mit dem Nachweis mehrerer QS BS Projekte QSS 2

• Fundierte Berufserfahrung in der chemische oder biologische Produktion Industrie von Vorteil

• Ausgezeichnete mündliche, schriftliche und zwischenmenschliche Kommunikationsfähigkeiten

• Ausgezeichnete organisatorische Fähigkeiten (Priorisierung in schnelllebiger Umgebung) sowie eine selbstständige und präzise Arbeitsweise

• Erfahrung im Umgang mit Behörden und Ämtern wünschenswert

• Deutsch (Muttersprache), Englisch (verhandlungssicher) von Vorteil

Lonzas Produkte und Services wirken sich täglich positiv auf Millionen von Menschen aus. Für uns stellt dies nicht nur eine grosse Ehre, sondern auch eine grosse Verantwortung, dar. Wie wir unsere Geschäftsergebnisse erreichen ist für uns genauso wichtig wie unsere Erfolge selbst. Bei Lonza wird Respekt grossgeschrieben und wir schützen sowohl unsere Mitarbeiter als auch unsere Umwelt. Erfolg für uns bedeutet moralisch vertretbarer Fortschritt.

Zu Lonza kommen Menschen, die Herausforderungen annehmen und mit ihrer Kreativität im Bereich Life Science neue Ideen für komplexe Problemstellungen entwickeln. Zusammen leisten wir einen Beitrag, der das Leben vieler Menschen auf der ganzen Welt verbessern kann. Dies bietet ihnen die Gewissheit und die Zufriedenheit, mit der eigenen Arbeit wirklich einen Unterschied zu machen.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.description:
Umzugsunterstützung: Verfügbar für berechtigte Kandidatinnen und Kandidaten sowie deren Familien.

Unser Standort in Visp, Schweiz wächst kontinuierlich und wir sind derzeit auf der Suche nach einem Operations Manager Launch Manufacturing (m/w/d). In dieser Position leitest  Du die Herstellung innovativer Wirkstoffe in unseren Produktionsanlagen . Wir in  Development Services & Small-/Mid-Scale Operations unterstützen unsere Kunden von ersten Machbarkeitsstudien, über die klinischen Prüfung bis zum Produktlaunch und der kommerziellen Fertigung. Wir entwickeln Produktionsverfahren für innovative Wirkstoffe in unseren Laboren und führen sie in unseren Betrieben nach GMP in technischem Massstab durch. Als Operations Manager Launch Manufacturing hast Du entscheidenden Anteil daran, dass Patienten weltweit neue Behandlungsoptionen geboten werden.  

Was auf Sie wartet:

• Eine agile Karriere und eine dynamische Arbeitskultur

• Ein integratives und ethisches Arbeitsumfeld

• Vergütungsprogramme, die hohe Leistung anerkennen.

Neben einem wettbewerbsfähigen Gehalt erwarten Sie zahlreiche Vorteile für Lifestyle, Familie und Freizeit. Unsere vollständige Liste der lokalen Benefits finden Sie hier:

• Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi 

Ihre Hauptaufgaben

• Du bist verantwortlich für die termingerechte, qualitativ hochwertige und kosteneffiziente Herstellung Deiner Produkte. Dabei hältst Du die geltenden Vorschriften und Richtlinien in Bezug auf Sicherheit, Qualität und Umwelt ein 

• Du leitest die Erstellung von Risikoanalysen, um Qualität und Sicherheit während der Produktion zu gewährleisten 

• Du identifizierst Möglichkeiten zur kontinuierlichen Verbesserung 

• Du koordinierst und leitest funktionsübergreifende Teams und stellst sicher, dass unsere Kunden kosteneffizient und pünktlich beliefert werden 

• Durch effektive Kommunikation, Leistungsbereitschaft und Teamfähigkeit schaffst Du ein vertrauensvolles und erfolgreiches Arbeitsumfeld, in dem wir und unsere Kunden ein gemeinsames Ziel verfolgen 

• Du passt Dich flexibel an sich ändernde Prioritäten an und bist in der Lage, gleichzeitig mehrere Projekte zu unterstützen

Ihr Profil

• Abgeschlossenes Studium (PhD oder Master) in Chemie, Chemieingenieurwesen, Verfahrenstechnik oder einer verwandten Fachrichtung

• Berufserfahrung in der chemischen und/oder pharmazeutischen Entwicklung oder Produktion von Vorteil

• Kenntnisse in GMP sowie betriebswirtschaftliche Kompetenz wünschenswert

• Ausgeprägte analytische Fähigkeiten und Freude an der Bearbeitung komplexer technischer Fragestellungen

• Fliessende Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift

• Hohe Team- und Kommunikationsfähigkeit im Umgang mit unterschiedlichen Stakeholdern und Hierarchieebenen

• Proaktive, flexible und lernbereite Persönlichkeit mit Verantwortungsbewusstsein und hoher Leistungsfähigkeit unter Zeitdruck

Über Lonza
Bei Lonza sind unsere Mitarbeiter unsere grösste Stärke. An über 30 Standorten auf fünf Kontinenten arbeiten unsere global vernetzten Teams täglich zusammen, um die Medikamente von morgen herzustellen. Unsere Grundwerte: Zusammenarbeit, Verantwortlichkeit, Exzellenz, Leidenschaft und Integrität spiegeln wider, wer wir sind und wie wir zusammenarbeiten. Die Ideen jedes Einzelnen, ob gross oder klein, haben das Potenzial, Millionen von Leben zu verbessern, und genau daran möchten wir Sie teilhaben lassen.
Innovation gedeiht, wenn Menschen unterschiedlichster Herkunft ihre einzigartigen Perspektiven einbringen. Bei Lonza schätzen wir Vielfalt und setzen uns für ein integratives Umfeld für alle Mitarbeiter ein. Wenn Sie bereit sind, die bahnbrechenden Ideen unserer Kunden in praktikable Therapien umzusetzen, freuen wir uns, Sie an Bord begrüßen zu dürfen.

Bereit die Zukunft der Life Sciences zu gestalten?
Jetzt bewerben

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

descriptionByLocale.it: Descrizione Moving Support: Disponibile per i candidati idonei e le loro famiglie. Vuoi introdurre la tua esperienza chimica in un ambiente di produzione moderno assumendo la responsabilità di qualità, sicurezza ed efficienza? Come Operazioni Chemist Manufacturing (m/w/d), parteciperai attivamente alla produzione di principi attivi farmaceutici e intermedi a Visp, in Svizzera e contribuirai in modo significativo ai processi produttivi stabili, sicuri e potenti. In questo ruolo, sei una parte centrale del programma di pianificazione per la qualità, il controllo e l'affidabilità e spingi avanti la riduzione degli errori sostenibili, la gestione dei rischi proattivi e il miglioramento continuo. # Cosa # Cosa ti aspetta: - Una carriera agile e una cultura di lavoro dinamica - Un ambiente di lavoro integrato ed etico - Programmi di Remunerazione che riconoscono alte prestazioni. Oltre a uno stipendio competitivo, troverete numerosi vantaggi per lo stile di vita, la famiglia e il tempo libero. Il nostro elenco completo delle prestazioni locali si trova qui: - Vantaggi in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi ## Conseguite i vostri compiti principali - Garantire una produzione tempestiva, qualitativamente perfetta e conveniente secondo gli standard di sicurezza, qualità e ambiente - preparazione e implementazione di analisi dei rischi e monitoraggio sicuro del vostro team attraverso l'intero processo - identificazione e implementazione di potenziali di ottimizzazione lungo la catena del valore In più di 30 sedi in cinque continenti, i nostri team di rete a livello globale lavorano insieme ogni giorno per produrre i farmaci di domani. I nostri valori fondamentali: cooperazione, responsabilità, eccellenza, passione e integrità riflettono chi siamo e come lavoriamo insieme. Le idee di ogni individuo, grande o piccolo, hanno il potenziale di migliorare milioni di vite, e vorremmo farvi partecipare. L'innovazione prospera quando le persone delle origini più diverse portano le loro prospettive uniche. A Lonza apprezziamo la diversità e siamo impegnati in un ambiente inclusivo per tutti i dipendenti. Se siete disposti a tradurre le idee innovative dei nostri clienti in terapie pratiche, siamo lieti di accogliervi a bordo. Pronto a plasmare il futuro delle scienze della vita? Lonza è leader mondiale nella produzione di farmaci e biotecnologie. L'azienda gestisce importanti stabilimenti produttivi a Visp (Valais), Basilea e Stein (Aargau), Svizzera.
descriptionByLocale.en: Descrizione Moving Support: Available for eligible candidates and their families. Would you like to introduce your chemical expertise in a modern production environment while taking responsibility for quality, safety and efficiency? As Operations Chemist Manufacturing (m/w/d), you will actively participate in the production of pharmaceutical active ingredients and intermediates at Visp, Switzerland and contribute significantly to stable, secure and powerful production processes. In this role, you are a central part of the Planning for Quality, Control and Reliability program and drive forward sustainable error reduction, proactive risk management and continuous improvement. ## Cosa offriamo What awaits you: - An agile career and a dynamic working culture - An integrative and ethical working environment - Remuneration programs that recognize high performance. In addition to a competitive salary, you will find numerous benefits for lifestyle, family and leisure. Our full list of local benefits is found here: - Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi ## Achieving your main tasks - Ensuring a timely, qualitatively perfect and cost-efficient production according to safety, quality and environmental standards - preparation and implementation of risk analyses and safe monitoring of your team through the entire process - identification and implementation of optimization potentials along the value chain At more than 30 locations on five continents, our globally networked teams work together daily to produce the drugs of tomorrow. Our basic values: cooperation, responsibility, excellence, passion and integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. The ideas of each individual, whether large or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and we would like to let you participate. Innovation thrives when people of the most diverse origins bring their unique perspectives. At Lonza we value diversity and are committed to an inclusive environment for all employees. If you are willing to translate our customers' groundbreaking ideas into practical therapies, we are happy to welcome you on board. Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

descriptionByLocale.fr: Descrizione Déménagement Support: Disponible pour les candidats admissibles et leurs familles. Souhaitez-vous introduire votre expertise chimique dans un environnement de production moderne tout en prenant en charge la qualité, la sécurité et l'efficacité? En tant qu'opérateurs de fabrication de chimistes (m/p/p), vous participerez activement à la production d'ingrédients actifs et d'intermédiaires pharmaceutiques à Viège, en Suisse, et contribuerez de manière significative à des processus de production stables, sûrs et puissants. Dans ce rôle, vous êtes un élément central du programme de planification de la qualité, du contrôle et de la fiabilité et dirigez la réduction durable des erreurs, la gestion proactive des risques et l'amélioration continue. Cosa offriamo Ce qui vous attend: - Une carrière agile et une culture de travail dynamique - Un milieu de travail intégratif et éthique - Programmes de rémunération qui reconnaissent le rendement élevé. En plus d'un salaire compétitif, vous trouverez de nombreux avantages pour le mode de vie, la famille et les loisirs. Notre liste complète des avantages locaux se trouve ici: - Avantages dans Viège: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi ## Réalisation de vos tâches principales - Assurer une production opportune, qualitativement parfaite et rentable selon les normes de sécurité, de qualité et d'environnement - Préparation et mise en oeuvre d'analyses de risques et de surveillance sûre de votre équipe tout au long du processus - Identification et mise en oeuvre de potentiels d'optimisation le long de la chaîne de valeur Dans plus de 30 sites sur cinq continents, nos équipes en réseau mondial travaillent ensemble tous les jours pour produire les médicaments de demain. Nos valeurs fondamentales : coopération, responsabilité, excellence, passion et intégrité reflètent qui nous sommes et comment nous travaillons ensemble. Les idées de chaque individu, petits ou grands, ont le potentiel d'améliorer des millions de vies, et nous aimerions vous laisser participer. L'innovation prospère lorsque les personnes d'origines les plus diverses apportent leurs perspectives uniques. Chez Lonza, nous apprécions la diversité et nous nous engageons à créer un environnement inclusif pour tous les employés. Si vous êtes prêt à traduire les idées révolutionnaires de nos clients en thérapies pratiques, nous sommes heureux de vous accueillir à bord. Prêt à façonner l'avenir des sciences de la vie? Postuler maintenant Lonza est un leader mondial dans la fabrication de produits pharmaceutiques et de biotechnologies. La société exploite d'importantes installations de production à Viège (Valais), Bâle et Stein (Argovie), Suisse.

jobs.title: HR Intern - Integrated Biologics 80-100%
jobs.description:
Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across five continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is talented people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of.

We are seeking a motivated and analytically curious Intern to join our HR Business Partner team. This internship offers a unique opportunity to gain exposure to strategic HR topics at the intersection of business performance, organizational effectiveness, and the future of work.
The intern will actively contribute to real HR initiatives, supporting data analytics, workforce lifecycle, engagement projects, and talent initiatives. This role is designed for someone eager to understand how HR drives measurable business impact.

What you will do:

Business & Talent Insights

• Assist in analyzing workforce data (performance, potential, retention risk, engagement) to derive actionable insights

• Contribute to the preparation of HR dashboards, executive summaries, and presentations to stakeholders

Future Ready & Organizational Development

• Conduct research on future of work trends (AI enablement, skills evolution, talent density, agile organizations)

• Support initiatives related to leadership capability building and culture transformation

• Help document and refine development journeys, stretch assignments, and capability frameworks

HRBP Partnership

• Attend and support team meetings; summarize discussions and track action plans

• Collaborate cross-functionally with HR Centers of Excellence and business stakeholders

• Contribute ideas to improve processes, employee experience, and organizational effectiveness

Operational Excellence

• Prepare reports, documentation, and presentation materials for leadership discussions

• Manage multiple priorities with structured time management

• Support administrative and coordination tasks with a continuous improvement mindset

What we are looking for:

• Currently pursuing a degree in HR, Business Administration, Organizational Behavior, or related field

• Strong analytical mindset with interest in business strategy and talent management

• Ability to navigate ambiguity and think in systems rather than tasks

• Strong verbal and written communication skills in English (German is a plus)

• Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel and PowerPoint required); experience in HR analytics tools is a plus

• High curiosity about leadership, organizational performance, and AI-driven transformation

• Positive attitude, ownership mindset, and willingness to challenge the status quo constructively

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

The intern will actively contribute to real HR initiatives, supporting data analytics, workforce lifecycle, engagement projects, and talent initiatives. This role is designed for someone eager to understand how HR drives measurable business impact.

What you will do:

Business & Talent Insights

• Assist in analyzing workforce data (performance, potential, retention risk, engagement) to derive actionable insights

• Contribute to the preparation of HR dashboards, executive summaries, and presentations to stakeholders

Future Ready & Organizational Development

• Conduct research on future of work trends (AI enablement, skills evolution, talent density, agile organizations)

• Support initiatives related to leadership capability building and culture transformation

• Help document and refine development journeys, stretch assignments, and capability frameworks

HRBP Partnership

• Attend and support team meetings; summarize discussions and track action plans

• Collaborate cross-functionally with HR Centers of Excellence and business stakeholders

• Contribute ideas to improve processes, employee experience, and organizational effectiveness

Operational Excellence

• Prepare reports, documentation, and presentation materials for leadership discussions

• Manage multiple priorities with structured time management

• Support administrative and coordination tasks with a continuous improvement mindset

What we are looking for:

• Currently pursuing a degree in HR, Business Administration, Organizational Behavior, or related field

• Strong analytical mindset with interest in business strategy and talent management

• Ability to navigate ambiguity and think in systems rather than tasks

• Strong verbal and written communication skills in English (German is a plus)

• Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel and PowerPoint required); experience in HR analytics tools is a plus

• High curiosity about leadership, organizational performance, and AI-driven transformation

• Positive attitude, ownership mindset, and willingness to challenge the status quo constructively

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

jobs.description:
Location: the actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

The QA Specialist (QA On The Floor) is responsible to the Associate Director, Head of QA Operations BioAtrium  in joining a strong and innovative team that is perceived as a main contributor to the success of the large scale mammalian operations in our future BioAtrium site.  A great candidate will have strong GMP mindset, have leadership capabilities, envisioning and driving teamwork, quality culture, and innovation with the goal of becoming a role model within Lonza. We are looking for someone comfortable working in a matrix organization, effective with cross-functional projects, someone who can conceptualize where we need to be, how to get there, and drive ideas to completion.  The QA Specialist will within the QA Operations team as QA On The Floor, but will support other QA Ops teams (e.g. Clean Utilities and Environmental Monitoring, QA for QC and Electronic Batch Record Review) They will interact with the the other Operational teams at BioAtrium like Production, Engineering and Project Management. 

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.
Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi  

What you will do:

• Comply with, Develop, implement and monitor quality procedures to ensure compliance with GxP standards, corporate policies, Swissmedic, FDA and other regulatory body regulations and guidelines.

• Support all QA operations activities concerning SOP landscape, electronic batch records, training,  deviation- / CAPA- and change control management

• Support all audits and inspections

• Identify and implement Lean / OPEX approaches in Quality and foster improvement projects

• Provide KPI reporting and execute action plans to address opportunities for improvement

• Work collaboratively with QA Operations and QA department colleagues

What we are looking for:

• Academic degree in a Life Science discipline/ Engineering/ Quality Management or related field

• Experience in working on a regulated Medical device, Drug product or Drug Substance site

• Experience with Lean / Operational Excellence / Six Sigma

• GMP compliance expertise

• Ability to interface and communicate directly with customers and regulatory bodies

• Excellent written and oral communication to include accurate and legible documentation

• Ability to work on call and/or out of normal working hours on site

• Fluency in English is required, German language skills are an advantage

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.
Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.
Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Project Lead Phys-Chem QC 80-100%
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Basel Stücki, Switzerland.

For our successful Drug Product focused branch located in Basel and Stein, Switzerland, we have exciting opportunities. Become part of a group that enables its customers to meet some of the greatest challenges in patient treatment and join us by applying for the R&D Project Lead position.

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture

• An inclusive and ethical workplace

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance

What you will do:

• Lead pharmaceutical development projects from early phases to market readiness

• Ensure timely delivery of QC work-packages and documentation

• Represent QC in internal and customer meetings

• Address risks proactively and communicate effectively

• Maintain compliance with cGMP and SOPs

• Support audits and act as SME for QC topics

• Drive process improvements and share best practices

What we are looking for:

• MSc or PhD in Analytical Chemistry, Biochemistry, Pharmaceutical Sciences or related field

• Experience in Analytics or Quality Control under cGMP regulations

• Knowledge of analytical lifecycle for biotechnology products and CMC requirements

• Strong communication and interpersonal skills with a proactive attitude

• Detail-oriented and able to work in a matrix organization

• Business fluent English, written and spoken

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together.
